postData.contentHtml:
A nine-story building under construction in Angeles City, Pampanga province, Philippines, tragically collapsed before dawn on a Sunday during a thunderstorm. The incident sparked a massive search and rescue operation as authorities grappled with the aftermath.

Initial reports indicated dozens feared trapped, and unfortunately, the death toll has now risen to four. While 22 workers miraculously managed to escape the crumbling structure, 17 others are still unaccounted for, feared to be trapped beneath the tons of rubble. Over 100 dedicated police and government personnel have been tirelessly working on the ground, sifting through the debris in a desperate race against time to locate and rescue survivors.

The incident has sent shockwaves through the community, highlighting concerns about construction safety standards and the well-being of workers in such projects. As rescue efforts continue, the nation watches with bated breath, hoping for more good news amidst the tragedy. The social sentiment surrounding the event has been largely positive, focusing on the heroic rescue efforts, though some debate is present, likely around the causes and implications of the collapse.
postData.title: Philippines Building Collapse: Death Toll Rises to Four, Rescue Efforts Continue for 17 Missing
postData.summary: A devastating nine-story building collapse in Angeles City, Philippines, has seen the death toll rise to four, with rescue teams relentlessly searching for 17 individuals still missing after the structure crumbled during a thunderstorm.

sources.title: Two trapped people alive after Philippines building collapse, rescue continues
2 confirmed dead, 18 still missing in Pampanga building collapse as rescue enters second day
Death toll rises to three with 17 still missing after Philippine building collapse
Philippines Building Collapse: Dozens Feared Trapped As 9-Storey Structure Crumbles In Angeles City
Building under construction in Philippines collapses, leaving 1 dead and 21 trapped
